programmes.show_description: From a teen's suburban bedroom to the heart of Silicon Valley, the story of a heartbroken father's bid for the truth behind his daughter's death, caught up in a powerful algorithmic spiral.
The Butcher of Suburbia: The landmark series brings us the gripping inside story of the murder of a 74-year-old woman - and the killer's macabre efforts to conceal her death. (Ep1/2)
The Butcher of Suburbia: The suspect admits to the murder of a 74-year-old woman. But a deeply disturbing tale that shocks experienced detectives has only just begun. (Ep2/2)
Some extra brunch as Tim Lovejoy and Simon Rimmer host the food and chat show.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
Kirstie and Phil catch up with cautious first-time buyers Sundeep and Rupreet in Harrow and postcode-picky Hilary and Rob, who set their hearts on Hitchin in Hertfordshire.
With planning permission granted, Keith and Marj start removing the pews, parquet floor and pulpit in the main hall, and make a discovery in the Sunday school ceiling vents.
In South Wales, Daffyd plans a nutritious vegetarian-ish dinner party, but kitchen chaos ensues. Will his dud pud ruin his chances of winning the grand? (Ep3/5)
The B&Bers visit Barbara and Elizabeth's boutique B&B in Norfolk, where the hosts try to dance away some doubts about grout with an old-school Wurlitzer organ.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In France, new plans keep Andrew and Richard on their toes. Best friends Janey and Sharon start an adventure. And will the floor-fillers fall flat at Abi and Darren's disco?

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programmes.show_description: Gerry and Kathy want to buy a holiday home by the sea, but can't agree on where it should be: the wild coastline of Pembrokeshire in Wales or the sunny Greek island of Zakynthos.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What
programmes.show_description: Happened Next?: How's Simon and Lawrence's renovation project in Charente going? And has Karen and Jerry's bolthole in Corfu proved the paradise they hoped it would be?
Salisbury. Natasha Conti throws a 'summer fete', with hilarious prizes, and asks her guests to dress in country casuals. But one guest finds himself in an awkward position. (Ep1/5)
Salisbury. Farm manager Sophie plans a spot of pig racing to entertain her guests. But how will they feel when she reveals that she is serving her home-reared cow Levi for the main? (Ep2/5)
Salisbury. Former army major Anthony serves a menu mash-up of cheesy quails' eggs, chicken plov and porridge ice cream. And with no dress code, Chris decides to suit up as a sailor. (Ep3/5)
Salisbury. Cost-conscious Chris Hiller plans an eclectic menu, including peppers stuffed with baked beans, and even waters down his wine to save cash. (Ep4/5)
Salisbury. Part-time care worker Jane Arnold plans an English menu sourced from the great Wiltshire outdoors, on a night featuring racy revelations and tears at the table. (Ep5/5)
Sue and Pete can't wait to start their new life in the Paphos region of Cyprus with their puppy Billy. Jasmine Harman helps them find a detached house with a pool for their £250,000 budget.
In Harrogate, Ilkley and northern Leeds, Kirstie and Phil help two young first-time buyers who are hoping to settle down, and a family who've been scarred by a previous purchase.
...Less: The Sellers family mealtimes are chaos thanks to dad Rudi's long working hours and daily post-commute supermarket trips. Will they ever get dinner on the table before 9pm?

programmes.show_name: Jamie: What to Eat This Week
programmes.show_description: Jamie Oliver takes us on a delicious journey as he re-awakens the spring kitchen garden after winter. He celebrates rhubarb in a lip-smacking sauce and heroes spinach in a risotto. (Ep1/3)
In Ho Chi Minh City, the most populous city in Vietnam, Bill is caught between wartime tunnels, endless crazy traffic, and a deep dive into a local musical twist of metal versus folk. (Ep2)
Documentary capturing the impact of the earthquake and tsunami that devastated parts of Japan in March 2011, using amateur footage filmed by those caught up in the disaster.
This time the Goggleboxers watch The Apprentice, Britain's Got Talent, Handcuffed: Last Pair Standing, Love Is Blind, Celebrity Puzzling, and Alexander Armstrong in India.
On the Gogglebox: Britain's Got Talent, The Heat, 56 Days, Olivia Attwood: Getting Filthy Rich, Ed Stafford's Rite of Passage, and Alice Roberts: Our Hospital Through Time.
Horse trainer Claire believes in country air and hard work. Her partner Bambi, a city-loving model and content creator, likes the easy life. Can Bambi keep up with Claire's schedule? (Ep3)
Can an eco-warrior and a petrolhead beauty salon owner agree to disagree on the environment? And a country-loving Yorkshireman shocks a London-based youth worker to the core. (Ep4)
...Experiment: Curious couples sex-periment with opening up their relationships. Connor and Kirstie explore having a threesome. Chanice and Richardo face an unexpected issue.
Comedian Adam Buxton and Joe Lycett peruse Prague's wonders, as they kayak down the River Vltava, sample sausages out of skulls and caviar-laced ice cream, and enjoy a beer spa.
Teleshopping.
Teleshopping.
In London, can Phil find Sam and Krystle a house before they get hitched? Kirstie helps tall twins Matt and Chris, who are clubbing together to buy their first home.
With the chapel still uninhabitable, Keith and Marj work on their first on-site bedroom: a shipping container in the car park!
The fourth host in South Wales, doctor Sophie, is usually vegan but is serving meat to try and win over her guests. Will it be just what the doctor ordered? (Ep4/5)
The week's final hosts, Helen and Carl, have their work cut out to impress at their Nottinghamshire glampsite after ruffling feathers with their feedback all week.

programmes.show_name: A New Life in the Sun
programmes.show_description: In France, renovations give Andrew and Richard sleepless nights. In Spain, things get a little too quiet for Abi and Darren, and Gordon and Shona's dream hangs in the balance.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programmes.show_description: With their children flying the nest, Andy and Alida want a smaller home in the UK and a chalet in the French Alps. Laura Hamilton searches in Berkshire, while Jonnie Irwin hunts in Morzine.

programmes.show_name: A Place in the Sun: What...
programmes.show_description: ...Happened Next?: Laura Hamilton visits Dawn and Steve at their dream town house in Mijas Pueblo. Ben Hillman catches up with Graham and Yvonne at their apartment in Alvor.
Huddersfield. First to host is cheeky mum-of-three Virginia Houslin, who hopes to wow her guests with her charm, flirtatious fun and some Caribbean home cooking. (Ep1/5)
Huddersfield. Business development manager Iyesha Atkins hosts a sophisticated dinner party. But will a discussion about religion derail her evening? (Ep2/5)
Huddersfield. Wedding singer Richard serves steak. But rather than asking his guests how they'd like theirs cooked, in the words of his hero Frank Sinatra, he's doing it 'my way'. (Ep3/5)
Huddersfield. Self-styled 'King of Clubs' Tommy Mack hosts a Hollywood Silent Movie night, with mysterious dishes and his own brand of rapping. (Ep4/5)
Huddersfield. Hairdresser Kim Connor plans an American diner-themed menu, with a little help from her mum, and some rock 'n' roll entertainment. (Ep5/5)
Jasmine Harman jets off to Kapparis in eastern Cyprus to help Bill and Sandra find their perfect two-bedroom holiday home for £80,000. Are any of Jasmine's properties up their street?
Kirstie and Phil help two sets of house-hunters who've relocated to Aberdeen for work and need to find new family homes fast.

programmes.show_name: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or...
programmes.show_description: ...List It: In Alnwick, Kirstie and Phil meet ministers Chris and Caroline, who need a divine intervention. Caroline loves their home and neighbours, but Chris craves more space.

programmes.show_name: Aldi vs M&S: Battle of the Brands
programmes.show_description: It's posh versus upstart. From cakes to wine, Kate Quilton explores the ultimate British supermarket showdown. This isn't just any supermarket turf war... this is Aldi vs M&S. (Ep2)
Scott's rushed into Queen's Medical Centre by air ambulance after crashing his motorbike at 100mph. And Peter has been crushed by an eight-tonne static caravan.
PC Jodie and PC Zoe are called to break up a brawl between neighbours. And veteran officer Emma struggles to identify a child who's found wandering the streets late at night.
A dangerous driver tries to flee the scene of a crash. An altercation between housemates needs some police attention. And Pete's copper's nose uncovers something unexpected.
